title: Signal Interruption
content:
{{Top|fa|rwm|imp}} {{Adventure |image=[[File:Signal_Interruption_title.jpg]] |title="Signal Interruption" |author=[[Jesse Decker]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20010809a|text=Signal Interruption}} |cover artist= |illustrator=*[[Adam Hughes]] *[[Sean Glenn]] (art direction) *[[Todd Gamble]] (cartography) *[[Kyle Stanley Hunter]] (graphic design) |editor=[[Sue Weinlein Cook]] |publisher=[[Wizards of the Coast]] |game=''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]'' |published in=[[Wizards.com]] |reprinted in= |release date=[[August 9]], [[2001]] |pages=5 |timeline=[[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]"Signal Interruption" describes itself as taking place during the events described at the beginning of {{Film|I}}, prior to the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. |campaign= |preceded by= |followed by= }} {{Quote|A new ''Star Wars'' adventure game scenario! For use with ''the Dark Side Sourcebook''|Description|}} "'''Signal Interruption'''" is a [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] web-exclusive [[roleplaying game]] adventure created for use with the ''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]'' by [[Wizards of the Coast]] and designed to introduce players to concepts found in ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''. The adventure was written by [[Jesse Decker]] and published on [[Wizards.com]] on [[August 9]], [[2001]]. "Signal Interruption" takes place on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] shortly before the events of the [[1999]] [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. The player characters meet with the covert [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] operative [[Raymas Daal]] and agree to [[Attack on the Kwilaan sensor array|attack]] a [[Naboo (people)|Naboo]] [[Sensor array (Kwilaan)|sensor array]]. The encounter is interrupted by the arrival of operatives of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]], which the players have to resolve. Afterwards, they complete the raid on the [[Sensor array/Legends|array]]. "Signal Interruption" was available on Wizards.com until [[2010]], when Wizards of the Coast chose not to renew their ''[[Star Wars]]'' license. ==Contents== {{Quote|Let's make this quick, I think I was followed.|Raymas Daal, to the player characters|}} [[File:Kwilaan_restaurant.png|thumb|right|250px|The map of the restaurant in Kwilaan]] The opening of "Signal Interruption" establishes that the adventure takes place immediately prior to the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] featured in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. The adventure portrays one of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] [[Saboteur/Legends|covert]] operations in the lead-up to the Trade Federation's landing on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The introduction specifies rules for playing the scenario, the pieces required to do so, and states that certain information should only be viewed by the gamemaster. It also specifies that the scenario descriptions assume that the players are using heroic characters who are sympathetic to the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]], while also offering suggestions for characters affiliated with the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. The adventure consists of three encounters. In "Encounter 1: This Is Only a Test," the player "heroes," in need of [[Currency/Legends|funds]], have arranged to meet with small-time criminal [[Raymas Daal]] in a [[Kwilaan restaurant|restaurant]] in [[Kwilaan (city)|Kwilaan]], a [[City/Legends|city]] on Naboo. After arriving there, the heroes find that the usually reliable Daal is not present. Just as they consider leaving, Daal rushes in, asking to resolve their business quickly, as he believes he was followed. He offers the group 8000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to temporarily [[Attack on the Kwilaan sensor array|deactivate]] a [[Sensor array (Kwilaan)|sensor array]] outside the city. Daal claims that he intends to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggle]] a shipment of rare [[Spice/Legends|spices]] to Naboo, hiding that his true purpose is to allow the Trade Federation to place [[Jammer/Legends|jamming]] [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]] without detection. The heroes then have the opportunity to ask questions about the raid and potentially negotiate a higher fee. [[File:Sensor_array_map.png|thumb|left|300px|The map of the sensor array]] In "Encounter 2: Do Not Attempt to Adjust Your Screen," the conversation ends when three plainclothes operatives from the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] enter the restaurant and ask Daal to join them for questioning for suspected Trade Federation affiliation. Daal runs to the building's exit, evading [[Blaster/Legends|blaster-fire]] from the operatives. One of them then turns his [[Blaster pistol/Legends|pistol]] on the heroes, and it is up to the players to resolve the encounter. The adventure gives instructions for how players need to set up the map and pieces for the encounter. "Encounter 3: Broadcast Signals" starts as the heroes approach the sensor array the following [[Standard day/Legends|night]] and have to decide if they will proceed by force or stealth. Four [[Naboo Palace Guard/Legends|Naboo Palace Guards]] protect the sensor array and are knocked out by the players. Inside the array, the heroes have to decide how to deal with the two [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] there before deactivating the array. Afterwards, they need to track down Daal to receive their payment. In the middle of Encounter 3, the adventure includes two maps for players to use. The scenario's conclusion suggests that the heroes can extend their adventures by investigating Daal further. It suggests that [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]]–aligned heroes may seek to undo the damage they have caused to Naboo, while dark side players could continue working for the Trade Federation and come to the attention of the orchestrator of the upcoming invasion, [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]].{{Film|I}} ==Development== {{Quote|This short adventure is designed to introduce beginning players and GMs to some of the exciting concepts found in the new Dark Side Sourcebook by Bill Slavicsek and JD Wiker.|Description on the Wizards of the Coast website|}} [[File:InvasionOfTheedGame.jpg|thumb|right|250px|"Signal Interruption" was intended to be used alongside the ''Invasion of Theed Adventure Game''.]] "Signal Interuption" is a five-page [[roleplaying game]] adventure created for use with [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game]]'', which had been released on [[November 22]], [[2000]].{{Wizards|url=news/pressrelease.asp?20001113a|text=Enter A Galaxy Far, Far Away With The New ''Star Wars'' Roleplaying Game}} The adventure was written by [[Jesse Decker]] and illustrated by [[Adam Hughes]]. Other credited personnel include [[Sue Weinlein Cook]] for editing and web production, [[Sean Glenn]] for art direction, [[Todd Gamble]] for cartography, [[Kyle Stanley Hunter|Kyle Hunter]] for graphic design, [[Leland Chee]] for [[Lucas Licensing]], and [[Thomas M. Reid]] as the creative director. The adventure was created to introduce players to concepts found in ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'', a supplement to the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' written by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[J.D. Wiker]] and published on [[August 2]], [[2001]].{{Wizards|url=News/pressrelease.asp?20010802a|text=Secrets Of The Dark Side Revealed In Distinctive ''Star Wars Roleplaying Game'' Sourcebook}} ==Release and reception== "Signal Interruption" was published as a PDF file on [[Wizards.com]] on [[August 9]], 2001, a week after the release of ''The Dark Side Sourcebook''. The webpage for the adventure included an additional PDF of its maps. The webpage also stated that players do not need a copy of ''The Dark Side Sourcebook'' to complete the storyline, while also recommending that having it—alongside content from the [[February]] 2001{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010131.html|text=Naboo's Secrets Revealed}} sourcebook ''[[Secrets of Naboo]]'' by [[Steve Miller]]—will be helpful. In [[2010]], Wizards of the Coast elected to not renew their ''[[Star Wars]]'' license. All content related to the franchise, including "Signal Interruption," was removed from their website in that year.{{SWArchive|url=games/othergames/news20100128wotc/index.html|text=An Announcement about Star Wars RPGs and Miniature Gaming}} ==Continuity== {{Quote|Do you think she suspects an attack?''"
"''I don't know, but we must move quickly to disrupt all communications down there.|Rune Haako and Nute Gunray in dialogue from ''The Phantom Menace'' that is partially explored in "Signal Interruption"|}} [[File:Trade_Federation_Droid_Army.png|thumb|left|350px|The events of "Signal Interruption" tie in to the Invasion of Naboo as depicted in ''The Phantom Menace''.]] "Signal Interruption" is described as taking place during events mentioned at the beginning of the [[1999]] [[prequel trilogy]] film ''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'', which takes place in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The adventure specifically reveals how the Trade Federation was able to jam Naboo's offworld transmissions during their invasion of the planet. The [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', released on [[November 11]], 2001,{{GameSpot|url=games/star-wars-galactic-battlegrounds|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}} also portrays the disruption of Naboo's communications through the [[subjugation of Parrlay]], in which the Trade Federation attacks and destroys several [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitters]] during their landing.''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' The city of Kwilaan shares its name with [[Kwilaan/Legends|Kwilaan]], a Naboo historical figure first mentioned in ''Secrets of Naboo''.''[[Secrets of Naboo]]'' The adventure uses character tokens included in the ''Invasion of Theed Adventure Game'', which include depictions taken from other ''Star Wars'' characters that appeared in the background of scenes from ''The Phantom Menace''. Daal is represented through the generic mercenary token, which uses the image of [[Televan Koreyy]],''[[Star Wars: The Phantom Menace: The Expanded Visual Dictionary]]'' while the RSF characters are represented by thug tokens, two of which depict [[Bok Askol/Legends|Bok Askol]] and one of which depicts [[Mat Rags/Legends|Mat Rags]]. The first page of "Signal Interruption" also depicts four of the hero characters from the ''Invasion of Theed Adventure Game'': [[Rann I-Kanu]], [[Arani Korden]], [[Galak]], and [[Rorworr (Naboo)|Rorworr]].[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]] The adventure instructions mention that the players can use these characters and the other heroes from the game, which include [[Dané]], [[Deel Surool]], [[Toba]], and [[Sia-Lan Wezz]]. However, there is no direct [[canon]]ical confirmation that these individuals were those responsible for the attack on the sensor array. The conclusion of the scenario refers to the originator of the Invasion of Naboo, confirmed to be Darth Sidious in ''The Phantom Menace'', as a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] rather than a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Daal was later mentioned in the [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. His entry established that after Daal hired freelancers to attack the sensor array, he was eventually captured by the Royal Naboo Security Forces.{{CSWECite|I|160|Daal, Raymas}} As a part of [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] media at the time of its publication, "Signal Interruption" was categorized as ''Star Wars'' Legends media in [[2014]].{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images
